name: Southeast Community Development Corporation
description_short: HUD-approved housing counseling agency in Southeast Baltimore offering free mortgage assistance, homebuyer education, and neighborhood revitalization programs.
description_long:
Southeast Community Development Corporation is a nonprofit organization based in Southeast Baltimore that focuses on promoting healthy, dynamic, and diverse communities through housing and neighborhood development initiatives. Founded on the principle that homeownership drives generational wealth, the organization has positioned itself as a comprehensive resource for residents seeking housing stability and community improvement. The organization operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency, which means it meets federal standards for providing unbiased housing advice. Southeast CDC serves multiple neighborhoods within Baltimore's Southeast region, with particular focus on areas including Highlandtown, East Baltimore, McElderry Park, and Ellwood Park.

Southeast CDC offers a range of free and low-cost services centered on housing and community development. Their primary offerings include HUD-approved housing counseling for homebuyers, free mortgage assistance for homeowners behind on payments (including mortgage, property taxes, water bills, and homeowners insurance), homebuyer education workshops, and a lender referral list to connect borrowers with appropriate financing options. The organization also purchases and rehabilitates vacant homes for sale at affordable prices in select neighborhoods. Additionally, they administer the Neighborhood Spruce-Up Grant program, which awards $7,500 to $20,000 to community-driven public space improvement projects. Services are available in both English and Spanish.

What distinguishes Southeast CDC is its hyperlocal focus on Southeast Baltimore neighborhoods and its integration of individual housing counseling with broader community revitalization efforts. Unlike national credit counseling agencies, this organization combines mortgage crisis intervention with proactive neighborhood improvement grants and affordable home sales programs. Their HUD approval status provides legitimacy and indicates compliance with federal housing counseling standards. The organization appears genuinely committed to equity, offering bilingual services and targeting neighborhoods with historically limited access to homeownership resources.

The main limitation is geographic scope—services are restricted to Southeast Baltimore neighborhoods within their BRNI (Baltimore Reinvestment Neighborhoods Initiative) area. The website provides limited detail on counseling outcomes, wait times, or specific success metrics. While their mortgage assistance and homebuyer counseling are free, the organization's affordable home sales are limited to select properties and neighborhoods. Prospective clients outside their service area or seeking broader financial counseling beyond housing would need to seek alternative resources.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency meeting federal standards for unbiased advice
Free mortgage crisis intervention for homeowners behind on payments, property taxes, water bills, and insurance
Bilingual services (English and Spanish) increasing accessibility for Latino residents
Purchases and rehabilitates vacant homes for sale at affordable prices ($150,000 examples cited)
Neighborhood Spruce-Up Grants program ($7,500-$20,000) for community-driven public space improvements
Integrated approach combining individual housing counseling with neighborhood revitalization
Hyperlocal expertise in Southeast Baltimore neighborhoods and community dynamics
cons: Geographic limitation—services only available in Southeast Baltimore's BRNI area neighborhoods
Limited public information on counseling outcomes, success rates, or client satisfaction metrics
Affordable home inventory appears limited (website mentions only select communities and properties)
Website lacks specific details on counseling wait times, appointment availability, or response time for mortgage assistance requests
No information provided about credit counseling, debt management, or financial services beyond housing-specific assistance
best_for: Southeast Baltimore homeowners behind on mortgage payments, property taxes, or water bills seeking free intervention
First-time homebuyers in Southeast Baltimore neighborhoods wanting HUD-approved education and lender referrals
Community groups and nonprofits in Southeast Baltimore seeking $7,500-$20,000 grants for neighborhood improvement projects
Spanish-speaking residents in Southeast Baltimore needing homeownership guidance and housing counseling
diagnosis: Southeast CDC is best for Southeast Baltimore residents experiencing housing instability or seeking homeownership education who value hyperlocal, community-rooted nonprofit support. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—services are only available within Southeast Baltimore's designated BRNI neighborhoods, making them unsuitable for residents in other Baltimore areas or outside the city.
services: Free mortgage assistance for homeowners behind on payments
Property tax and water bill payment assistance for homeowners in crisis
Homebuyer education workshops with registration and certification
Lender referral list connecting borrowers to appropriate financing options
Affordable home purchase program (buying and rehabbing vacant properties)
Community-driven public space improvement project support
McElderry Park and Ellwood Park specific community planning services
Neighborhood revitalization project planning and implementation support
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
